topics.title: Basic Sentence Structure & V2 Rule
topics.explanation:
Swedish follows Subject-Verb-Object (SVO) order in simple sentences, similar to English.

The V2 rule: In main clauses, the verb must always be in the SECOND position. If the sentence starts with something other than the subject, the subject moves after the verb.

• Jag äter bröd. (I eat bread) – SVO
• Idag äter jag bröd. (Today eat I bread) – V2

Swedish has two genders: en-words (common) and ett-words (neuter).
• en bok (a book) – boken (the book)
• ett hus (a house) – huset (the house)

quiz.question: What special rule applies to Swedish main clauses?
Swedish has how many grammatical genders?
